name: Guinea
experiences: Voile de la Mariée waterfall near Kindia
Fouta Djallon treks, waterfalls, and Fulani highland scenery
Mount Nimba Strict Nature Reserve UNESCO add-on
avoid: June to October is the hardest window: Fouta Djallon trails get muddy, landslides slow bush taxis, and pirogues to the islands are much less predictable.
Domestic flights inside Guinea are limited and irregular, so build the core trip around road transport and treat flights as an occasional time-saving bonus rather than a fixed plan.
Outside larger Conakry hotels, card acceptance drops fast; keep cash ready for bush taxis, waterfall access contributions, pirogues, and guides in Kindia and Fouta.
stopoverNote: Conakry (CKY) is the only practical international gateway. The smoothest loop is Conakry city first, an Îles de Los day trip or overnight by pirogue, then a road climb through Kindia into Fouta Djallon; only check domestic flights if you are adding far-east Guinea such as N'zérékoré or Mount Nimba.

cities.name: Conakry
cities.notes: Atlantic capital for Madina market chaos, museum stops, city beaches, and the boat departures that make the rest of coastal Guinea work.

cities.name: Fouta Djallon (Dalaba / Labé)
cities.notes: Highland base split between Dalaba and Labé for Fulani culture, cool air, waterfalls, forest walks, and the classic Guinea trekking days.

cities.name: Îles de Los
cities.notes: Short pirogue hop from Conakry for Kassa and Roume beaches, seafood lunches, and a quick island reset without leaving the capital region.

cities.name: Kindia
cities.notes: Lowland gateway toward Fouta Djallon, best known for Voile de la Mariée, Mount Gangan viewpoints, and simple overnight stops out of Conakry.

itinerary.sim: Orange Guinée is the safest all-round pick for Conakry, Kindia, and the main Fouta towns, while island coverage can dip after you leave the harbor; budget roughly ₹480–₹1,440 for a starter SIM or eSIM with enough data for maps, WhatsApp, and booking confirmations.

days.theme: Ease into Guinea with Kaloum logistics, museum context, and the Atlantic-facing neighborhoods that make Conakry feel busiest right away.

activities.name: Airport taxi from Ahmed Sékou Touré International Airport to Kaloum, Camayenne, or Kipé
Musée National de Sandervalia entry or donation
Marché Madina browse and snack stop
Taxi rides between Kaloum, Camayenne, and Taouyah/Kipé
Sunset walk on Plage de Camayenne or Plage de Taouyah

days.theme: Use a second city day for gardens, practical errands, and keeping one buffer before road or boat schedules start dictating the trip.

activities.name: Conakry Botanical Garden walk
Port de Boulbinet taxi run to reconfirm Îles de Los pirogue timing
Guinea National Museum or artisan shopping revisit
City taxi hops between Kaloum, Madina, and the Corniche-style coast roads
Beach chair or drink stop by Kipé

days.theme: Cross to Kassa or Roume for the easiest Guinea island day: pirogue timing, seafood, and a proper break from Conakry traffic.

activities.name: Shared pirogue round-trip from Port de Boulbinet to Kassa or Roume
Island landing or beach contribution when collected
Kassa beach walk and swim near Kassa Kounki Hôtel-Restaurant-Bar
Shared boat hop between Kassa and Roume when available
Kayak, snorkel, or informal beach gear rental

hotels.name: La Villa Elijah - Guinea Fishing Camp

days.theme: Keep an extra island night only if the sea is calm enough to justify a slower Tamara or Roume loop before you return to the mainland road trip.

activities.name: Private or semi-private pirogue for a Kassa-Roume-Tamara loop
Tamara lighthouse and village walk
Seafood lunch and drinks on Roume or Kassa
Return pirogue back to Conakry if sea state delays the morning departure
Beach hammock or shaded rest setup

hotels.name: La Villa Elijah - Guinea Fishing Camp

days.theme: Break the climb inland with Kindia's classic waterfall stop and a simple overnight that still feels distinctly greener than Conakry.

activities.name: Bush taxi or shared car from Conakry to Kindia
Voile de la Mariée waterfall entry
Shared taxi or moto from Kindia town to Voile de la Mariée
Mount Gangan local guide or lookout contribution
Kindia market walk and tea stop

days.theme: Use a second night only if you want an easier transition day for Kilissi scenery before the longer haul into Fouta Djallon.

activities.name: Les Eaux de Kilissi walk or picnic stop
Short taxi around Kindia and nearby gardens
Local snack or juice break
Bush taxi stand run to pre-book the Fouta leg
Sunset rest back at the hotel grounds

days.theme: Climb into the highlands, settle around Dalaba, and take a first easy day for cool air, old villas, and pine-shaded walks.

activities.name: Bush taxi or shared car from Kindia to Dalaba
Villa Sylvia visit or caretaker donation
Les Sapins forest walk
Taxi or moto around Dalaba town and viewpoints
Evening tea stop overlooking the Tangama side of town

days.theme: This is the core Fouta day: trekking, waterfall time, and the kind of guide-led rural walking that makes Guinea's highlands worth the long road.

activities.name: Local guide for a Pont de Dieu or Cascade de Ditinn full-day trek
Waterfall or village access contribution
Shared moto or hired car to the trailhead
Packed lunch and trail snacks
Optional second short waterfall stop if transport timing holds

days.theme: Shift toward Labé for markets and bigger mountain views, using the day for one more scenic push before heading back toward Conakry.

activities.name: Shared taxi from Dalaba or Pita toward Labé
Dame de Mali or Mount Loura local guide share
Labé central market walk
Moto or taxi to viewpoints outside Labé
Evening tea and grilled dinner back in town

days.theme: Keep a fourth highland day only if roads are behaving well enough for another waterfall, market, or slow return setup before the long ride home.

activities.name: Second local guide half-day for a shorter ridge or village walk
Bush taxi station booking for the Conakry return
Short waterfall or craft stop close to town
Local café and bakery stops
Flexible rest block for weather or road delays

connections.method: Shared pirogues from Port de Boulbinet are the standard move; leave buffer because departures wait for enough passengers and sea conditions
Return by pirogue to Conakry first, then switch to a bush taxi or shared car inland toward Kindia
Bush taxis and shared cars are the normal highland transfer, with rougher timing once rain arrives
Road returns by bush taxi are the norm, while limited domestic flights from Labé only make sense as an occasional backup when a schedule actually operates

extras: November to April is the dry-season sweet spot: roads into Kindia and Fouta are easier, waterfall trails are safer, and pirogues to the islands run with fewer weather cancellations.
Voile de la Mariée is a cheap, fast win out of Kindia; its small gate fee and taxi ride make it one of Guinea's best-value nature stops.
Fouta Djallon trekking costs are mostly guide and vehicle driven rather than ticket driven, so group up when possible to soften the ₹1,920–₹3,840 full-day guide bill.
Mount Nimba Strict Nature Reserve is a separate far-east add-on around Nzérékoré and Bossou; expect about ₹960–₹2,880 per day for permit-plus-guide arrangements before transport.
Îles de Los is best as a flexible weather day rather than a fixed last-minute airport connection, because pirogues can wait to fill or cancel with rougher sea.
Cash is still king almost everywhere outside Conakry's bigger hotels, especially for bush taxis, moto rides, small waterfalls, and guide tips.
